# Research Summary: app-store-submission-stinger

**Generated by:** scripture-historian (Phase 1.5)  
**For:** `app-store-submission-worker-bee` Bee  
**Date:** 2026-05-20

---

## Research metadata

- **Depth tier consumed:** normal
- **Time window covered:** 2025-11-01 to 2026-05-20 (6 months)
- **Files written:** 14 (1 research-plan.md + 1 index.md + 12 external/ source notes)
- **Source types:** official-docs (5), practitioner blogs (7), practitioner guide (2)
- **Queries executed:** 8 (5 primary + 3 expansion)
- **Research tool:** Exa web_search_exa

---

## Five most influential sources for stinger-forge

### 1. Push My App: App Store Rejection Reasons Index (2026)
**File:** `external/2026-05-20-app-store-rejection-reasons-index.md`  
**Why it matters:** Provides 80+ real-world rejection codes observed in 2026 with precise guideline citations. The key insight: Apple vs Google rejection philosophies diverge sharply. Apple rejects on quality/design/metadata accuracy; Google rejects on data-safety declarations and permissions policy compliance. This asymmetry must drive how `guides/03-rejection-playbook.md` is structured. Also contains the single most practically useful fact about PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy: the rejection almost always comes from a third-party SDK missing its manifest, not the app target.

### 2. Google Play Policy Announcement: April 15, 2026 (Official)
**File:** `external/2026-05-20-google-play-policy-updates-april-2026.md`  
**Why it matters:** Three new policies with compliance deadlines of October 28, 2026 will trip up any Android developer who hasn't read the announcement. Contacts permissions now require Android Contact Picker; precise location requires the location button; geofencing has been removed as an approved foreground services use case. These are breaking changes for apps in those categories. This is primary compliance material for `guides/02-compliance-checklist.md`.

### 3. Mobile App Wiki: Privacy Manifest Requirements (2026)
**File:** `external/2026-05-20-privacy-manifest-requirements-2026.md`  
**Why it matters:** The most operationally complete guide to PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy enforcement as it stands in 2026. Lists all five required-reason API categories (file timestamp, system boot time, disk space, user defaults, active keyboard), explains that reasons must be selected from Apple's predefined list (no custom reasons), and provides the pre-flight verification checklist that can be adapted directly into the stinger's `templates/` folder. This is the compliance backbone for `guides/02-compliance-checklist.md`'s iOS section.

### 4. Swift Crafted: StoreKit 2 SwiftUI Setup Guide (2026)
**File:** `external/2026-05-20-storekit-2-swiftui-guide-2026.md`  
**Why it matters:** Definitive production-ready StoreKit 2 guide updated for iOS 26 and Swift 6.2. Contains the five critical production patterns that prevent the most common StoreKit bugs (never hardcode prices, finish transactions after granting access, start listener at app entry, handle grace period, always provide restore button). Also confirms StoreKit 1 formal deprecation at WWDC 2024 and introduces SubscriptionOfferView (iOS 26+) and appTransactionID. Essential source for `guides/04-iap-setup.md`.

### 5. AppStoreReview: Review Queue Delays in 2026
**File:** `external/2026-05-20-app-store-review-times-2026.md`  
**Why it matters:** Data-backed analysis explaining that review times are systematically longer in 2026 due to a 60-80% YoY increase in app submissions (104% in April 2026). New baseline: 2–5 days for new apps, 24–72 hours for updates. Expedited review is being approved less frequently. This source grounds the timeline guidance in `guides/05-timeline-and-process.md` with current evidence rather than Apple's stated 24-hour benchmark, which is no longer reliably achieved for new app submissions.

---

## Five open questions for stinger-forge

1. **EU DMA IAP options: when does the "no mix & match" rule apply exactly?**  
   The research confirms that EU apps under Apple's Alternative Terms cannot offer both App Store IAP and an alternative PSP "on the same storefront," but the exact definition of storefront scope (per country? per platform?) is not completely clear. Stinger-forge may need to fetch Apple's Alternative Terms Addendum PDF for the precise definition.

2. **Expedited review hard limit: "twice a year" or just a soft cap?**  
   The Monexapps source says developers get "only twice a year," but the AppStoreReview source says Apple "tracks your history" and will start denying if used frequently, without specifying a hard limit. The Apple developer documentation page only says "extenuating circumstances." Stinger-forge should flag this uncertainty and recommend treating expedited review as a scarce resource regardless of the exact limit.

3. **Google Play Billing Library current version: is PBL 8.x production-ready?**  
   The research covers PBL 7 migration thoroughly (official docs) but PBL 8.x (cited in one migration logic reference) is mentioned without detailed documentation. Stinger-forge should check `developer.android.com/google/play/billing/release-notes` for the current stable version before finalizing `guides/04-iap-setup.md`.

4. **What are Apple's specific requirements for AI-generated content disclosure in 2026?**  
   Multiple rejection sources mention "AI-generated content disclosures" as a 2026-specific rejection category (the irisapp.cc source explicitly lists it as item 13). The research does not contain a source that specifies exactly what Apple requires. Stinger-forge should fetch the App Review Guidelines section on AI-generated content or request a targeted follow-up research sweep.

5. **Does the new Google Play account transfer policy (May 27, 2026) affect existing pending transfers?**  
   The policy announcement is clear on future transfers, but the research does not clarify whether teams in the middle of informal account transfers before May 27, 2026 are grandfathered or immediately in violation. Stinger-forge should add a note in the compliance guide recommending teams complete any pending transfers through the official process before that date.

---

## Sources stinger-forge should re-fetch with deeper context

- **Apple App Review Guidelines (full text):** `developer.apple.com/app-store/review/guidelines/` - The research uses secondary citations of guideline numbers. Stinger-forge should fetch the current guidelines directly to confirm all cited section numbers are still accurate (Apple occasionally renumbers sections with major reviews).
- **App Store Connect Help > Privacy:** `help.apple.com/app-store-connect/` - Operational steps for completing privacy declarations. The research has the conceptual layer but not the step-by-step App Store Connect UI workflow.
- **Google Play Developer Policy Center (full text):** `play.google.com/about/developer-content-policy/` - Current full policy text for cross-referencing the April 2026 updates against the base policy.
- **RevenueCat Blog:** The Command Brief specifically cited RevenueCat as an aggregated community intelligence source for ASO and IAP patterns. The research did not reach a RevenueCat source; stinger-forge should check `rev.cat/blog` for 2026 IAP configuration guides.
